page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-8 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical Intel Xeon processor cores running at 2.3 GHz, with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is configured with 7.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 0.9 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the n1-highcpu-8 delivers modest processing capabilities. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU performance, along with Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores, fall into the weak to poor categories. However, the instance demonstrates average performance in Passmark CPU scores, Geekbench ray tracing, and large-block uncached memory bandwidth read and write operations. Conversely, tasks such as gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and text processing register in the bottom 10% of benchmarked systems.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n1-highcpu-8 offers a high density of dedicated vCPUs relative to its memory footprint, making it a viable option for CPU-bound tasks that do not require large memory allocations. Due to its low RAM-to-vCPU ratio and weak database and Redis benchmark results, it is poorly suited for memory-intensive databases or high-throughput web serving. It is best utilized for lightweight parallel processing, software compilation, and basic computational workloads where memory overhead is minimal.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 7.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz. This configuration provides a low memory-to-core ratio of 0.9 GB per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators included. Performance benchmarks place the instance mostly in the weak to poor categories for single-core and multi-core operations, though it achieves average marks in Passmark CPU testing, ray tracing, and large-block memory bandwidth. Given its low memory allocation and dedicated CPU resources, the n1-highcpu-8 is qualitatively cost-efficient for CPU-bound tasks with minimal memory requirements, such as basic software compilation and lightweight parallel processing, while remaining less suitable for memory-heavy databases or high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 7.0 GB of RAM, designed for CPU-bound workloads with low memory requirements.
